transcript: fluid is this lubricant like substance that's found in between your joints. It kind of looks like an egg yolk. So when you stretch out your joint you are releasing gas and that gas forms a bubble and it collapses and pops. In order to crack the same knuckle again you have to wait about 20 minutes for the gas to return back to that fluid. So how is that different from a pop you hear when you stand up quickly? The sound you're probably hearing then is the snapping sound.
